suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: haiku_stream
formats.instruction: Express your idea or reaction as a series of three haikus, each representing a different perspective or mood. Strict 5-7-5 syllable format. No explanations.

formats.name: dialogue_duel
formats.instruction: Write a short, dramatic conversation between two fictional characters. They must debate or explore a single topic with distinct personalities. Minimum four exchanges, max one sentence per character per turn.

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare your stance on a niche issue using bold, punchy statements. Begin with 'I believe...' and follow with three rapid-fire beliefs. No fluff.

formats.name: time_travel_letter
formats.instruction: Write a letter to your future self (or from your future self to now), detailing predictions, advice, or urgent warnings about a specific trend. Imaginative, yet practical tone.

formats.name: question_cascade
formats.instruction: Chain five thought-provoking questions about your topic, each building on the last. No answers or statements. Let curiosity drive the structure.

formats.name: visual_snapshot
formats.instruction: Describe a real or imagined scene that encapsulates your point. Use vivid sensory details and paint a picture in the reader's mind. No direct analysis; show, don't tell.

formats.name: what_if_wormhole
formats.instruction: Start with 'What if...' and dive deep into an alternate reality scenario. Explore practical consequences, emotional impacts, or surprising outcomes in a speculative essay.

formats.name: grocery_list_brainstorm
formats.instruction: Write a topic brainstorm as a literal shopping list, with each item representing a point, concern, or idea. Use playful item names. End with an unexpected 'receipt total'—a single summary line.

formats.name: mythbusting_riddle
formats.instruction: Frame your topic as a riddle that uncovers a common misconception. End with a clever punchline or twist that resolves the riddle and reveals the truth.

title_styles: Write an enigmatic title that leaves the reader itching for the answer. Examples: 'The invisible thread connecting X and Y', 'What happens when Z meets its match?'
Write a humorous, meme-style title. Examples: 'X be like: Y', 'If X had feelings, it would be Y'
Write a poetic, metaphor-driven title. Examples: 'Chasing fireflies in the data jungle', 'Sailing through the fog of X'
Write a challenging title that reverses expectations. Examples: 'Everything you've heard about X is wrong', 'Why Y isn't the villain most people think'
Write a short question as a title. Examples: 'Is X really worth it?', 'What did we miss about Y?'
structure_variants: Write as alternating point-counterpoint paragraphs, as if debating with yourself.
Use a polaroid structure: present flashes of memory or imagery in brief, unconnected paragraphs.
Begin with a personal anecdote, then branch into universal lessons or takeaways.
Build your post as a countdown, starting at 5 and ending at 1, with each section decreasing in scale or scope.
Write in fractured sentences and fragments; let the ideas tumble out, raw and unedited.
topic_angles: Explore everyday micro-moments of digital life nobody notices.
Analyze the future of commuting post-remote work.
Dissect the hidden economics of online attention spans.
Imagine how today's youth will remember social media in 30 years.
Unpack surprising parallels between animal behavior and tech culture.
